title: viola × caesariensis
intro: Viola × caesariensis, also known as the hybrid pansy or alpine pansy, is a hybrid plant species in the violet family (Violaceae) resulting from the cross between Viola alpina and Viola lutea. Traditionally, Viola × caesariensis has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving anxiety and stress, and acting as an antiviral agent. In herbal preparations, Viola × caesariensis is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical creams and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
